'''Harrison Luther Mata'afa Hansen''' (born 26 October 1985) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} and {{rlp|lf}} for [[Baroudeurs de Pia XIII]] in the French domestic [[Super XIII]] league. He has played for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at international level.

He has also played for the [[Wigan Warriors]], [[Salford Red Devils]], [[Leigh Centurions]] and the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]]. During his time with Wigan, he won two [[Super League Grand Final]]s and two [[Challenge Cup]]s.

==Background==
Hansen was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand.

==Early life==
Hansen is the son of the rugby league footballer for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] and [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]], Shane Hansen. He is a product of the Folly Lane ARLFC who play at the Blue Ribbon Field, [[Pendlebury]].<ref>The Blue Ribbon Field is in Pendlebury, not Swinton. It is bounded by the railway line, Pendlebury Road, Fraser Street, Heron Street and Hilton Square. PLEASE DO NOT CHANGE. THANK YOU.</ref> Hansen attended the same [[Swinton, Greater Manchester|Swinton]] school, [[Moorside High School, Swinton|Moorside High School]], as [[Manchester United]]'s [[Ryan Giggs]]. He is of [[Samoans|Samoan]], Chinese, and Scottish descent.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2008/oct/30/rugbyleagueworldcup2008-tongarugbyleagueteam|location=London|work=The Guardian|first=Andy|last=Wilson|title=Hansen goes to war for his Samoan ancestors|date=30 October 2008}}</ref>

Hansen impressed in Wigan's Academy setup and was signed on a new two-year deal in July 2003.

==Club career==
Hansen continued his development by being promoted to the first team setup for [[2004 Super League season|Super League IX]]. He made his first team début in [[Terry O'Connor (rugby league)|Terry O'Connor]]'s Testimonial match against [[London Broncos]]. He went on to make six substitute appearances in the season.

In 2005, Hansen became a more established first team regular in the second row, partly due to the departure of [[Andy Farrell]], and injuries to [[Sean O'Loughlin]] and [[Gareth Hock]]. He signed a new two-year contract with the club in April 2005, and an extended four-year deal with Wigan on 14 November 2006, keeping him at the club until 2010.

Hansen played in the [[2010 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Wilson|first=Andy|title=Wigan turn St Helens into sinners with Super League Grand Final win|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/oct/02/wigan-st-helens-grand-final|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=The Guardian|date=2 October 2010}}</ref>

Hansen's 2011's [[Super League XVI]] started with a try against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] during the season-opening [[Magic Weekend]] event, which was followed up with another try against [[Bradford Bulls]] the week after.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/9393298.stm|title=St Helens 16–16 Wigan|date=12 February 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|title=Bradford 10–44 Wigan|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/9400211.stm|access-date=5 May 2011|publisher=BBC|date=20 February 2011}}</ref> Hansen scored again in Round 3's match against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]], however in Round 12 during a victory against [[Wakefield Trinity]], he suffered an injury which would keep him out of action for at least a month.<ref>{{cite news|title=Warriors battle past Reds|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,19936,11070_3343362,00.html|access-date=5 May 2011|publisher=Sky Sports|date=4 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|title=Wigan ease past Wildcats|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,19936,11070_3343418,00.html|access-date=5 May 2011|publisher=Sky Sports|date=25 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Hadfield|first=Dave|title=Farrell to fill Hansen's boots for Wigan|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/farrell-to-fill-hansens-boots-for-wigan-2277388.html|archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/20220509/https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/farrell-to-fill-hansens-boots-for-wigan-2277388.html|archive-date=9 May 2022|url-access=subscription|url-status=live|access-date=5 May 2011|newspaper=The Independent|date=1 May 2011|location=London}}</ref>

Hansen played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward in the [[2011 Challenge Cup Final]] 28–18 victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Scott|first=Ged|title=Leeds 18–28 Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14670204|access-date=21 November 2019|publisher=BBC Sport|date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/aug/27/leeds-rhinos-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup|location=London|work=The Guardian|first=Andy|last=Wilson|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–28 Wigan Warriors – Challenge Cup final match report|date=27 August 2011}}</ref>

Hansen played in both the [[2013 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23794947|title=Hull FC 0–16 Wigan Warriors|date=24 August 2013|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/aug/24/hull-fc-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup-final|title=Wigan Warriors overcome Hull FC and elements to win Challenge Cup|date=24 August 2013|work=The Guardian|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,,11070_3660839,00.html|title=Wigan Warriors grind out victory over Hull|date=24 August 2013|publisher=Sky Sports|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref> and the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal|publisher=Super League|access-date=14 July 2013|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130729050226/http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal|archive-date=29 July 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|publisher=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131007163603/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead|archive-date=7 October 2013|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10358228/Super-League-Grand-Final-2013-Warrington-Wolves-16-Wigan-Warriors-30-match-report.html|title=Warrington Wolves 16 Wigan Warriors 30|date=5 October 2013|work=The Daily Telegraph|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title=Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=The Guardian|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

In 2013, Hansen signed for [[Salford Red Devils]] for an undisclosed fee, signing a four-year contract.<ref name="HansenSalford">{{cite news|url=http://www.thereddevils.net/news/rugby-news/red-devils-sign-harrison-hansen-from-warriors|title=Red Devils sign Harrison Hansen from Warriors|date=17 December 2013|publisher=Salford Red Devils Official Website}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/9077757/rugby-league-harrison-hansen-leaves-wigan-for-salford-red-devils|title=Rugby League: Harrison Hansen leaves Wigan for Salford Red Devils|date=17 December 2013|publisher=Sky Sports}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/25419139|title=Salford: Harrison Hansen leaves Wigan to join Red Devils|date=17 December 2013|publisher=BBC}}</ref> Upon signing, he claimed it was the influence of [[Marwan Koukash]] that meant he was signing, and that joining Salford would be "a great new challenge. I have achieved everything I could at Wigan and had a great time there. I'm not just coming to Salford for an easy ride".<ref name="HansenSalford"/>

=== Toulouse Olympique ===
On 18 September 2019, [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] announced that Hansen had left them and signed a one-year contract with Toulouse for the 2020 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://widnesvikings.co.uk/harrison-hansen-joins-toulouse/|title=Harrison Hansen Joins Toulouse|date=18 September 2019}}</ref>

On 10 October 2021, Hansen played for Toulouse in their victory over [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the [[Million Pound Game]] which saw the club promoted to the [[Super League]] for the first time in their history.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12430948/million-pound-game-toulouse-promoted-to-super-league-after-beating-featherstone#:~:text=Toulouse%20beat%20part%2Dtimers%20Featherstone,preferred%20French%20team%20in%202003/|title=Toulouse promoted to Super League after beating Featherstone|publisher=Sky Sports}}</ref> Three days later Toulouse announced that Hansen had signed a one-year contract extension until the end of the 2022 season.<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/a-la-une/harrison-hansen-rempile-un-an-de-plus-au-to/|title=Harrison HANSEN rempile un an de plus au TO|date=19 October 2021}}</ref>
Hansen played 20 games for the club in their [[2022 Super League season|2022 Super League]] campaign as they finished bottom of the table and were relegated back to the RFL Championship.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/toulouse-suffering-untold-damage-due-to-relegation-from-super-league/|title=Toulouse suffering 'untold damage' due to relegation from Super League|website=loverugbyleague.com|date=21 October 2022}}</ref>
On 15 October 2023, Hansen played in Toulouse Olympique's upset loss in the [[Million Pound Game]] against the [[London Broncos]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67105947|title=Championship Grand Final: Toulouse 14–18 London Broncos – Capital club back in Super League|publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref> On 19 October 2024, Hansen played in Toulouse's second successive Championship [[2024 RFL Championship|grand final]] defeat.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/articles/cqjr5lvxk4lo|title=Jowitt breaks points record as Wakefield cruise to Grand Final win|publisher=BBC Sport|date=19 October 2024|access-date=20 October 2024}}</ref>

===Baroudeurs de Pia XIII===
On 31 October 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Baroudeurs de Pia XIII]] in the [[Super XIII]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Wigan Today|date=31 October 2024|access-date=1 November 2024|author=Josh McAllister|title=Wigan Warriors icon Harrison Hansen reveals next career move in opportunity he couldn't turn down|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-icon-harrison-hansen-reveals-next-career-move-in-opportunity-he-couldnt-turn-down-4847900}}</ref>

On 6 June 2026, Hansen played in the 2025–26 Super XIII final in which Pia won 31–30 against [[AS Carcassonne]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Fages kicks Pia to Glory |work=[[Rugby Leaguer & League Express]] |p=24 |issue=3535 |date=8 June 2026}}</ref>

==Representative career==
Eligible for [[England national rugby league team|England]], New Zealand and Samoa, Hansen represented England at youth level. However, in 2006 he was called up into the New Zealand squad for the mid season international against Great Britain. He later switched his allegiance to Samoa, and made his Samoa début in 2007.

Hansen was part of the [[Samoa national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Squad|Samoan]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm|title=Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads|publisher=BBC|date=8 October 2008|access-date=9 October 2008}}</ref><ref name="Samoa name World Cup Squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=16750|title=Samoa name World Cup Squad|publisher=League Unlimited|date=5 August 2008|access-date=6 August 2008|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110717060051/http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=16750|archive-date=17 July 2011}}</ref>

In 2009 Hansen was named as part of the [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoan]] side for the [[2009 Pacific Cup|Pacific Cup]].<ref>[http://www.therhinos.co.uk/news/7999.php Poching names strong Samoan squad] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091010154654/http://www.therhinos.co.uk/news/7999.php |date=10 October 2009 }} ''therhinos.co.uk'', 6 October 2009</ref>

Following the withdrawal of [[Roy Asotasi]], Hansen was named Samoa's captain for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{usurped|[https://web.archive.org/web/20131022210930/http://www.rlwc2013.com/rugby-league-world-news/article/1183/warrior-hansen-to-lead-samoa Page Title]}}</ref> However just before the tournament kicked off, he suffered an injury, forcing him out of the tournament. He handed the captaincy over to [[Iosia Soliola]].
